req.title: Transfer funds between own accounts (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall transfer up to $25,000 per day between their own accounts; transfers above this shall be declined. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The violation was in test[2]: kind=negative but heuristic=BVA is disallowed for negative tests. The one-cent-over-limit test probes the boundary just above the allowed maximum, which is a boundary value concern. The fix is to change kind from 'negative' to 'boundary' for that test, which is semantically correct: $25,000.01 is the first value outside the valid partition and is a classic BVA upper-boundary+1 probe. All other tests are preserved exactly as before.

tests.name: test_transfer_within_daily_limit_succeeds
preconditions: Source and target accounts belong to the same customer
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=$1000, valid source/target, unique Idempotency-Key
expected: Response body contains transfer_id and status=completed
trace: R-FIN-078: customer transfers up to $25,000 per day
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_within_daily_limit_succeeds(api_client, own_accounts, reset_daily_transfers):
    src, tgt = own_accounts
    src_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(src)
    tgt_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(tgt)
    payload = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": 1000.00, "currency": "USD"}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": str(uuid.uuid4())}
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "completed"
    assert api_client.get_balance(src) == src_bal_before - 1000.00
    assert api_client.get_balance(tgt) == tgt_bal_before + 1000.00

tests.name: test_transfer_at_exact_daily_limit_succeeds
preconditions: Source and target accounts belong to the same customer
trace: R-FIN-078: up to $25,000 per day (inclusive upper boundary)

tests.name: test_transfer_one_cent_over_daily_limit_is_declined
preconditions: Source and target accounts belong to the same customer
expected: Response body error code indicates daily_limit_exceeded
trace: R-FIN-078: transfers above $25,000 shall be declined with 422
R-FIN-078: BVA upper boundary + 1 cent probes first rejected value
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_one_cent_over_daily_limit_is_declined(api_client, own_accounts, reset_daily_transfers):
    src, tgt = own_accounts
    src_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(src)
    tgt_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(tgt)
    payload = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": 25000.01, "currency": "USD"}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": str(uuid.uuid4())}
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    assert resp.json().get("error_code") == "daily_limit_exceeded"
    assert api_client.get_balance(src) == src_bal_before
    assert api_client.get_balance(tgt) == tgt_bal_before

tests.name: test_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409
preconditions: Source and target accounts belong to the same customer
expected: Response body error code indicates insufficient_funds
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409(api_client, own_accounts_low_balance, reset_daily_transfers):
    src, tgt = own_accounts_low_balance
    src_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(src)
    tgt_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(tgt)
    payload = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": 500.00, "currency": "USD"}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": str(uuid.uuid4())}
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    assert resp.json().get("error_code") == "insufficient_funds"
    assert api_client.get_balance(src) == src_bal_before
    assert api_client.get_balance(tgt) == tgt_bal_before

tests.name: test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_result
preconditions: Source and target accounts belong to the same customer
actions: POST /transfers/internal with identical payload and Idempotency-Key=KEY-001 (duplicate)
expected: First request returns HTTP 200 or 201 with transfer_id T1
Second request returns HTTP 200 with same transfer_id T1 and same status
Balance reflects only one debit of $500 (not two)
trace: R-FIN-078: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key must return original result
tests.executable:
def test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_result(api_client, own_accounts, reset_daily_transfers):
    src, tgt = own_accounts
    src_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(src)
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": 500.00, "currency": "USD"}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idem_key}
    resp1 = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code in (200, 201)
    transfer_id = resp1.json()["transfer_id"]
    resp2 = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp2.status_code == 200
    assert resp2.json()["transfer_id"] == transfer_id
    assert resp2.json()["status"] == resp1.json()["status"]
    assert api_client.get_balance(src) == src_bal_before - 500.00

tests.name: test_transfer_declined_when_cumulative_daily_total_would_exceed_limit
preconditions: Source and target accounts belong to the same customer
Customer has already transferred $20,000 today (daily total = $20,000)
expected: Response body error code indicates daily_limit_exceeded
trace: R-FIN-078: transfers above $25,000 per day shall be declined
R-FIN-078: DT combination of existing daily usage plus new request exceeding limit
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_declined_when_cumulative_daily_total_would_exceed_limit(api_client, own_accounts, seed_daily_transfers_20000):
    src, tgt = own_accounts
    src_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(src)
    tgt_bal_before = api_client.get_balance(tgt)
    payload = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": 5000.01, "currency": "USD"}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": str(uuid.uuid4())}
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    assert resp.json().get("error_code") == "daily_limit_exceeded"
    assert api_client.get_balance(src) == src_bal_before
    assert api_client.get_balance(tgt) == tgt_bal_before
